programme.desc: After a career serving all over the world, army veteran Jason, his wife Esther, and their four children have finally put down roots - but the trouble is their Suffolk garden is little more than a concrete car park. As the military is a big part of this family's life, they want to see the resulting garden incorporate some remembrance poppies. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Stowmarket to see how they can help Jason and Esther to realise their dreams(n)

Edith is finding her irritating sister Joan very difficult to accommodate, and Roger, impatient to see Phil sell his house, decides to help the process along by checking his roof for dry rot. Wendy drops in on Edith and mentions that she is planning a children's book, and despite not yet knowing what the plotline will be, thinks it may involve a complicated family. programme.desc: During the summer wedding season, Claire McCollum celebrates faith and marriage by visiting a couple in Cheshire whose wedding was fully organised by a church for just £1,000 thanks to Christian volunteers from the congregation. The Rev Kate Bottley meets members of the United Reformed Church, who three years ago voted to allow same-sex marriages in their churches, and a Christian same-sex couple who are able to marry in church as a result of this change(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the 11th meeting of the Diamond League season, which comes from Alexander Stadium in Birmingham. Great Britain's Laura Muir will be looking for another victory in the 1500m after prevailing last time out in London, and American sprint star Christian Coleman is expected to feature in the 100m, having pipped home favourite Reece Prescott to the line last year(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman will be hoping famously sharp minds will provide equally sharp answers as they host an academia-themed edition of the general knowledge quiz, in which points are earned by guessing the least likely correct answers given to questions posed to members of the public. Today's teams are made up of punk-rock poet John Cooper Clarke, an honorary doctor of the University of Salford, and historian Suzannah Lipscomb, a Lincoln and Baliol alumnus. TV archaeologist Mark Horton, a professor at University of Bristol, teams up with documentarian Alice Roberts, Professor of Public Engagement in Science at the University of Birmingham. Space scientist Maggie Aderin-Pocock joins forces with theoretical physicist Jim Al-Khalili of the University of Surrey, and University of Reading's Professor Kate Williams allies with The Infinite Monkey Cage comedian Robin Ince, who shares an alma mater with Kate in the form of Royal Holloway, University of London(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits the Lake District to take a look at some of the jobs working animals do. He joins the Cockermouth Mountain Rescue team to see search-and-rescue dogs in action and meets 15-week-old Jura, a border collie pup learning the ropes from the older dogs. He then meets George Newton and his two Dales ponies, Charlie and George. They work the forests for timber as they're able to get to places that normal machinery can't. But Sean is surprised to find that the biggest job is being done by the smallest creatures - beetles(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Elizabeth and Sean need builders to repair a DIY bodge job on their fireplace in Stretford, while first-time buyer Sam wants his tired kitchen in Battersea transforming(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Chef Ainsley Harriott and Strictly Come Dancing judge Len Goodman's road trip takes the pair to south London, where Len has his first taste of Portuguese and Japanese cuisine. Ainsley heads to the kitchen and cooks a mouth-watering Portuguese chicken inspired by the day's adventures(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the popularity of takeaway food in Britain and discuss the various ways that it has evolved over the years. The culinary duo also rustle up some of their favourite dishes(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Equine vet Sybil has to contend with a Highland cow in Teesdale, Co Durham, while treating Fell ponies, and Steve judges a dog competition at the local Bowes Agricultural Show. Meanwhile, Angela has to break bad news to the owner of a Rottweiler at Fife's small animal hospital. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts explore creatures' surprising physical characteristics, including how one man's understanding of an eagle's physical abilities could help save it from extinction. Conservationist Giles Clark witnesses an Australian kangaroo that is more at home climbing trees than hopping through the Outback, zoologist Lucy Cooke is in Costa Rica to find out if the sloth's laziness is the key to its success, and marine biologist Patrick Aryee reveals the secrets of one of the world's oddest looking creatures - the platypus(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Louise Gostelow head to the final auction in Brighton. Louise ends up in a beret and, with brush in hand, recreates a very old form of English art. Meanwhile, dog lover Phil learns about the history of some the country's favourite felines. At the last auction, which will attract the biggest bid - Phil's silver boxer dog or Louise's bronze puppy?(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Anna Williamson, Gavin Ramjaun, Martel Maxwell, Rav Wilding and Paul Rose form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The customers unbox, test and score a baby bib and tray kit, a smart mirror and a seaweed oil. But only one can win the Customer is Always Right trophy(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull head to Wales and discover a strong industrial engineering history, sailing across Pontcysyllte Aqueduct, the highest navigable aqueduct in the world. The tiny village of Hawarden gives the duo the opportunity to stay overnight in the UK's only residential library, before they make an attempt on the summit of Mount Snowdon itself - via the mountain's iconic rack-and-pinion narrow gauge steam railway train(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites more contestants to brave the black chair to answer question on specialised subjects including the Mughal - or Mogul - Empire, the life and legacy of physician and suffragist Elizabeth Garrett Anderson and Rod Serling's sci-fi series The Twilight Zone. They are then each afforded two minutes to demonstrate their prowess on general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon, so together with friend Paul Whitehouse, he sets off for the River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They stay in the heart of the Highlands, where Bob cooks Paul a traditional Scottish breakfast and enjoy a swift round of golf before resuming their fishing. The duo also reflect on luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives and later assess whether the trip has lived up to their expectations(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As Longmeadow radiates colour in the height of summer, Monty Don takes a range of cuttings to get plants for free and also shows how best to prune lavender. Carol Klein visits a garden in Devon to revel in its eryngiums, while the programme also meets a passionate gardener and his partner in Wiltshire who use their garden as inspiration for art(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The year 1973 finds Bob juggling dealing with the success of Cabaret, spearheading production on Liza with a Z and Pippin and grappling with the price of fame. Though he is indulging in the high life, with several girlfriends, pills and poppers, he finds time to attend the opening night of Gwen's Broadway play Children! Children! Gwen tearfully tells him that the play is closing after only one performance, since the producers have had word of bad notices and poor box office. She implies that if Bob had helped with rewrites, the production might have been saved. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Matt Lucas, Holly Walsh and Susan Calman join regular panellist Alan Davies, as question master Sandi Toksvig opens her post in a post-themed edition. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Edinburgh Nights with Nish Kumar
programme.desc: The comedian, actor, and presenter goes behind the scenes to sample The Blind Boys of Alabama in a remarkable partnership with Malian duo Amadou and Mariam. My Land, the Hungarian show that was top-rated of all the shows at Edinburgh last year, lets Nish behind the scenes to see the participants on the high wires pushing the boundary of what the human body is capable of. Nish also gets to know some of this year's up-and-coming comedians(n)

programme.title: In the Heat of the Night
programme.desc: When a wealthy industrialist is murdered in Mississippi, the bigoted local sheriff is quick to accuse a black newcomer to town - only to learn his suspect is a highly respected homicide detective from Philadelphia. However, the duo are forced to put aside their mutual loathing and team up to track down the killer. Oscar-winning drama, starring Sidney Poitier, Rod Steiger and Warren Oates(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's betting industry is booming, with the amount people gamble having almost doubled in a decade and company profits soaring to £14.5 billion. But are the bookies doing enough to protect problem gamblers? Reporter Bronagh Munro investigates an industry where complaints are rising even more quickly than profits, meeting one person who gambled away more than £3 million, and families who have lost their children to addiction.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Secret World of Incels

programme.title: Penguins on a Plane: Great Animal Moves
programme.desc: The conclusion of the documentary about exotic animals being transported around the world follows three saltwater crocodiles being moved to their new home at a zoo in Oxfordshire from France, where successful breeding has resulted in too many of them living in one enclosure. A 6ft bowmouth ray shark at Birmingham's National Sea Life Centre has to be caught and moved into a bespoke tank to travel 125 miles to Blackpool, and in Hungary, specialist wildlife transporter Matt Ford tries all the tricks of the trade to lure two timid arctic wolves into crates, so they can make the 1,600-mile journey to the Five Sisters Zoo in Scotland(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present a new format for the contest. Among the new twists are a new hazard that fills the arena with fog, Robot Redemption rounds, in which losing teams have a chance to get back into the contest, and the show's biggest ever battle, the Ten Robot Rumble, in which 10 teams battle to the death with no time limit(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond and a team of volunteers give the valais blacknose sheep a bath, Kirsty and Tom rescue fallen sheep on the isle, and Stevie attempts to milk his first buffalo. In Shetland, Kirsty and Tom again row out to St Ninian's Isle to attempt to rescue a ewe and a lamb that have fallen from the isle's steep cliffs(n)

programme.title: A Cook Abroad
programme.sub-title: A Cook Abroad: Rachel Khoo's Malaysia
programme.desc: The cook and food writer goes on a culinary tour of the Southeast Asian country. She begins in the capital Kuala Lumpur, gaining her first insights into Malaysian food and culture from family members she hasn't seen since childhood. Rachel then meets cooks and students at her father's old school and begins to find out why Malaysian food is so complex, before she heads to Penang, the food capital of the country, and discovers why Malaysia is a great place to be young, female and Muslim(n)

programme.title: It's a Mad, Mad, Mad, Mad World
programme.desc: A motley assortment of characters wreaks havoc across America in the search for loot from a botched bank robbery - but the only clue to the whereabouts of the fortune is the cryptic final phrase uttered by a car crash victim. Comedy, starring Spencer Tracy, Milton Berle, Dick Shawn, Jonathan Winters, Phil Silvers, Terry-Thomas, Peter Falk, Ethel Merman, Jimmy Durante. and Buster Keaton(n)

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin and the team continue to explore the archipelago, much of which still remains a mystery to science. The presenter ventures far beneath the waves in a deep-sea submersible in search of an elusive ocean giant, the mola or sunfish, to understand more about its little-known behaviour. She also sees how sea lions have been affected by the El Nino weather phenomenon, before the team sails north for the most remote and inhospitable islands in the Galapagos - Wolf and Darwin, where Liz helps tag and track hammerhead sharks that school at this location(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef lands in the town that placed Tequila on the map and explores Guadalajara, the city that gave the rest of the world Mariachis and dishes like Carne con Chilli. He also can't resist a visit to Casa Kimberly, bought by Richard Burton for Elizabeth Taylor, and where they still serve her favourite cocktail, a Chocolate Martini(n)

programme.title: Song for Marion
programme.desc: A grouchy old man has always been dismissive of his wife's role in the unconventional local choir. When she is diagnosed with a terminal illness and is no longer able to sing, he reluctantly agrees to take her place, a decision that dramatically changes his life and helps him through the hard times to come. Comedy drama, with Terence Stamp, Gemma Arterton, Vanessa Redgrave and Christopher Eccleston(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham presents the classical music magazine show to accompany the BBC Proms, from a bandstand directly opposite the Royal Albert Hall. This evening she explores the role of the conductor, with the help of leading maestros Mirga Gražinyte-Tyla, Peter Edwards and Lloyd Coleman. Lloyd also discusses conducting styles with Martyn Brabbins, the music director of the English National Opera. Plus, comedian and keyboard player Rachel Parris finds out what it is like to play Queen Victoria's own piano with Stephen Hough(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In the final episode of the series, the six baby animals approach the end of their first year on Earth. In Iceland, an Arctic fox cub rejected by his mother must learn where to find food for the first time, while in Kenya, spotted hyena twin sisters must roam a territory they share with the biggest threat to their lives, lions. Whether breaking ties or building bonds, all the young animals must learn for themselves how to stay healthy and safe(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: In an attempt to leave the men free for front-line fighting, Mainwaring and Wilson decide to recruit women into the platoon to take care of the more tedious tasks such as polishing brass. However, the captain loses his heart to an attractive widow, and it is not long before gossip spreads. Comedy first broadcast in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier, with a guest appearance by Carmen Silvera ('Allo 'Allo!)(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Made in Scotland
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary following the acclaimed comedian, actor and musician, as he returns to the country of his birth and reveals the influences and motivations that helped him move on from being a welder in Glasgow to become 'The Big Yin'. The programme features deeply personal interviews with Billy himself, alongside contributions from some of the greatest names in comedy and popular culture, who talk about Connolly's lasting influence(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Made in Scotland
programme.desc: Second of a two-part documentary following the comedian, as he returns to the country of his birth and reveals the influences and motivations that helped him become 'The Big Yin'. The programme finds Billy back in the Scotland of his childhood, where he reveals that knitted woolly swimming trunks were not a figment of his imagination. Featuring contributions by friends, fans and colleagues including Eddie Izzard, Ross Noble, Micky Flanagan, AL Kennedy, Tracey Ullman, Val McDermid, Sharleen Spiteri and Eddi Reader(n)

programme.title: Quartet
programme.desc: Three former opera singers prepare for their annual concert to mark Verdi's birthday and raise funds for their retirement home. However, this year, a famous diva has joined the group, and her history with the others threatens the performance. Comedy drama, directed by Dustin Hoffman and starring Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins and Michael Gambon(n)

credits.actor: Maggie Smith, Billy Connolly, Tom Courtenay, Pauline Collins, Michael Gambon

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Cellmates Fletcher and Godber are coerced into aiding an escape attempt organised by the sinister Grouty. However, the two jailbirds contrive to break back into prison before their absence is detected by the formidable Mr Mackay. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale, Peter Vaughan, Fulton Mackay and Brian Wilde(n)

credits.actor: Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale, Peter Vaughan, Fulton Mackay, Brian Wilde

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden heads to Aberdeenshire in her hot-air balloon as her journey continues. She visits Crathes Castle, an ancient seat of Scottish nobles, where she learns about the challenges of gardening in an unforgiving climate, before meeting Doug, whose father left his mark on the estate. She also gets to grips with some smelly skunk cabbage at what is claimed to be Britain's highest garden, Tillypronie(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As Longmeadow radiates colour in the height of summer, Monty Don takes a range of cuttings to get plants for free and also shows how best to prune lavender. Carol Klein visits a garden in Devon to revel in its eryngiums, while the programme also meets a passionate gardener and his partner in Wiltshire who use their garden as inspiration for art(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team is in Perthshire, where Matt Baker visits Cultybraggan, a former PoW camp that is now a thriving community of artisans, craftspeople and food producers. Charlotte Smith jumps in a kayak and takes to the water on a wildlife safari, while Steve Borwn meets women growing tea in the south Highlands and learns the art of making the perfect cuppa(n)

programme.title: The Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: George, Brian and Carole attempt to answer as many viewers' gardening questions as they can in a quickfire round dealing with Scotland's top 10 pests and diseases for 2019. Brian visits the Field Project in Dunkeld, an all-inclusive community project including people of all ages and backgrounds(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Sonia Harwood is a mother-of-three and foster carer who would love to create homemade family meals, but ends up relying on frozen food. Lorraine shows her how easy it can be to cook with fresh ingredients, preparing dishes including courgette fritters, a quick seafood linguine and a baked ricotta cheesecake(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater: Eating Together
programme.desc: The food writer takes a culinary tour of Britain. Starting with old-fashioned suet dumplings, he travels to Lincolnshire to try some pea kachori and lamb samosas that have a profound personal significance. Nigel then meets a man from Poland to learn the art of Pierogi making, as well as a woman whose traditional ravioli keeps a little bit of Italy alive for her family in Bedford(n)

programme.title: Top of the Shop with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Four sweetmakers with fledgling businesses get their chance to test their products on locals in Malhamdale, North Yorkshire, competing for a place in the final. Wajeeha hopes her chocolate bars can tap into the healthier-eating market, court officer Gillian brings two cocktail flavours of marshmallow to sell in the shop, Leonie presents her chocolate liqueurs and truffles, and Justin's Scottish tablet is from his grandmother's old recipe(n)

programme.title: Ulster GP Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the prestigious motorcycling event stages on the closed-off public roads of the 7.3-mile Dundrod Circuit in Lisburn(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Adam Partridge value items brought to Muncaster Castle, near Ravenglass in Cumbria, while Paul Martin finds out about the Lake District's bobbin industry(n)

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Choreographer Arlene Phillips heads to Southport, Merseyside, with presenter Len Goodman to relive memories from one of her favourite childhood holidays in 1954. The pair visit her auntie and uncle's old house where the family used to stay, eat the types of food she would have eaten as a youngster, take a ride on the town's pier train and spend some time on the beach(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is on a property-searching mission spanning Somerset and Devon's Exmoor National Park with a couple who have £550,000 and a love of carpentry and the coast(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Pangolins - The World's Most Wanted Animal
programme.desc: Documentary examining attempts to save the most illegally trafficked animals in the world - a little-known scaly mammal called the pangolin. Based in Namibia, conservationist Maria Diekmann rescues and rehabilitates these local creatures. Cameras follow her to Vietnam, Thailand and China - into the heart of the crisis, where demand for pangolin products is greatest(n)

programme.title: Oz the Great and Powerful
programme.desc: An amoral circus illusionist is transported to a magical land, where he is mistaken for a powerful wizard prophesied to fall from the sky and save the realm from evil. His attempt to take advantage of the situation leads him into a dangerous quest involving three powerful witches. Fantasy adventure prequel to The Wizard of Oz, starring James Franco, Mila Kunis, Rachel Weisz and Michelle Williams(n)

credits.actor: James Franco, Mila Kunis, Rachel Weisz, Michelle Williams

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a croissant factory in France that produces 336,000 pastries a day, and discovers how an 83-year-old strain of yeast is used to create the perfect flavour. Cherry Healey talks to a professor who specialises in exploring human senses about the perfect way to eat a croissant and visits a farm that produces concentrated butter that is 99.8 percent fat. Plus, Ruth Goodman is in Paris investigating the croissant's surprising Austrian origins and the role of bread in the French revolution(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani consider ideas from a pair of porridge-makers with a teddy bears' picnic-themed breakfast, two students who have some new ideas for an established marketplace, an Irish inventor who made a breakthrough with the help of his father, and an entrepreneur with a new approach to weight loss involving wearing more clothes(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan spends a week exploring Colombia with a local radio host and journalist, discovering how much of the country's reputation for violence is justified. He investigates a new trend for narcotourism, where visitors seek out places associated with drug lord Pablo Escobar, and finds out how Colombians feel about foreigners' fascination with their nation's most infamous criminal. He also visits a Jesus statue shop, learns to make coffee the traditional way, and discovers a game involving explosions(n)

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Sketch show, with Freya Parker and Celeste Dring, featuring characters from their live shows, including adventures of a whimsical French heroine, created by a man who has never met a woman(n)

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An anxious young woman attempts to cope with the two personifications of her inner thoughts - one which encourages her to be impulsive and another that fears everything. Written by and starring Gbemisola Ikumelo(n)

programme.title: A Girl's Guide to TV
programme.desc: Comedian Rachel Parris presents a comic guide on how women can get ahead in television, despite in her words 'their bodies teeming with pesky oestrogen'(n)

programme.title: When Bridges Collapse: The Genoa Disaster
programme.desc: On August 14, 2018, the Ponte Morandi in Genoa collapsed, sending 27 vehicles plummeting to the valley below with the loss of 43 lives. This investigation into the disaster tells the stories of people on the bridge that day and examines what caused the road viaduct to fail so catastrophically(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.sub-title: BBC Proms: Pappano and the National Youth Orchestra USA
programme.desc: Suzy Klein introduces America's most talented young musicians in an ambitious concert from the National Youth Orchestra of the USA. Antonio Pappano conducts the UK premiere of Benjamin Beckman's new work, Occidentalis and mezzo-soprano Joyce DiDonato sings Berlioz's beautifully crafted miniatures Les nuits d'été. The impressive programme culminates with Richard Strauss's epic work, An Alpine Symphony(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Simon Mayo introduce the edition first aired on May 12, featuring performances by Harry Enfield, Prince, the Adventures, Narada, Liverpool FC, Belinda Carlisle, Derek B, Prefab Sprout, Star Turn on 45 Pints, Wet Wet Wet, Fairground Attraction and Kylie Minogue(n)

programme.title: Woodstock - Three Days That Defined a Generation
programme.desc: In August 1969, over 400,000 people from all walks of life converged on a small dairy farm in upstate New York to hear a pop concert. However, most experienced something far more profound - a moment that came to define a cultural revolution. Through the voices of those who were there, the music of the time and unique imagery shot in situ of the protagonists and festivalgoers, this documentary tells the story of the lead up to those three historic days, and those extraordinary moments when the concert, almost pulled off right at the last minute, put the ideals of the counterculture to the test(n)

programme.title: Jimi Hendrix: The Road to Woodstock
programme.desc: A definitive account of one of the guitarist's most celebrated performances, at the New York festival in 1969, featuring signature renditions of Purple Haze, Voodoo Child and the Star Spangled Banner. The programme includes interviews with Woodstock promoter Michael Lang and Hendrix's band members Mitch Mitchell, Billy Cox, Larry Lee and Juma Sultan(n)

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Having spent over 25 years in the music industry, agent Emma Banks has worked with some of the world's most famous artists and seen first-hand the fine line between success and failure. The secret to success and stardom is an elusive formula of luck, timing talent as well as the team behind the talent - the record executives, label bosses and A&R gurus. Here, Emma offers a behind-the-scenes insight into how the industry finds talent and creates superstars. Contributors include Motown legend Martha Reeves, Blur's Alex James and Labelle's Nona Hendryx(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Simon Mayo introduce the edition first aired on May 12, featuring performances by Harry Enfield, Prince, the Adventures, Narada, Liverpool FC, Belinda Carlisle, Derek B, Prefab Sprout, Star Turn on 45 Pints, Wet Wet Wet, Fairground Attraction and Kylie Minogue(n)

programme.title: The Fall: The Wonderful and Frightening World of Mark E Smith
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the Fall, the enigmatic British post-punk band fronted by Mark E Smith, who died last month. Emerging from the Manchester punk scene during the late 1970s, the Fall underwent numerous style and line-up changes, with the only persistent member being frontman Smith, whose acerbic wit and biting lyricism became one of the band's few constant trademarks. Featuring footage of the group's last Peel Session, and contributions by Paul Morley, Tony Wilson, Stewart Lee and Franz Ferdinand(n)

programme.title: Singer-Songwriters at the BBC
programme.desc: An hour-long compilation of classic singles highlighting the growing popularity of the singer-songwriter genre during the 1970s, which became one of the decade's defining styles. Featuring music by Elton John, James Taylor, Cat Stevens, Harry Nilsson, Neil Diamond, Steve Goodman, Joni Mitchell and Neil Young(n)

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: The team pushes deeper into the rainforest on the tropical island of New Guinea, where cameraman Gordon Buchanan enlists the aid of local tribespeople to help him film birds of paradise performing their unusual courtship ritual. George McGavin has to contend with a giant crocodile, and Steve Backshall discovers a hidden network of caves never previously seen by humans(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand is now home to some of the richest agricultural land in the world, boasting magnificent gardens with plants from around the planet and rare magical creatures. A caving expedition reveals relics of the original lost world of its pre-human past; the actual bones of a cow-sized flightless grazing bird, a moa, among the largest birds that has ever lived. However, as narrator Sam Neill reveals, against all the odds, one giant bird from primeval New Zealand has survived to this day(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: Yusuf's fury is unleashed on June and Philip when he discovers she has not given him her correct mobile number. Rami's behaviour becomes increasingly reckless. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in the forest, with June's fingerprints on the murder weapon, while Yusuf uses Philip's phone to demand a plane. In Danish(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: Hardliners in Hanoi seize the initiative and send combat troops to the south, accelerating the insurgency, while US president Lyndon Johnson escalates America's military commitment(n)

programme.title: The Vietnam War
programme.desc: Defying American airpower, North Vietnamese troops and materiel stream down the Ho Chi Minh Trail into the south, while Saigon struggles to pacify the countryside. As an antiwar movement builds in the US, American soldiers discover that this conflict is nothing like the one their fathers fought(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Simon Mayo introduce the edition first aired on May 12, featuring performances by Harry Enfield, Prince, the Adventures, Narada, Liverpool FC, Belinda Carlisle, Derek B, Prefab Sprout, Star Turn on 45 Pints, Wet Wet Wet, Fairground Attraction and Kylie Minogue(n)

programme.title: Sings Musicals
programme.desc: Memorable versions of songs taken from or inspired by musicals. The programme features Ella Fitzgerald singing Mack the Knife, from the Threepenny Opera, Captain Sensible's 1982 chart-topper Happy Talk, a cover of a song from South Pacific, and performances by Jeff Beck and Jay-Z(n)

programme.title: Lost Land of the Volcano
programme.desc: The team pushes deeper into the rainforest on the tropical island of New Guinea, where cameraman Gordon Buchanan enlists the aid of local tribespeople to help him film birds of paradise performing their unusual courtship ritual. George McGavin has to contend with a giant crocodile, and Steve Backshall discovers a hidden network of caves never previously seen by humans(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand is now home to some of the richest agricultural land in the world, boasting magnificent gardens with plants from around the planet and rare magical creatures. A caving expedition reveals relics of the original lost world of its pre-human past; the actual bones of a cow-sized flightless grazing bird, a moa, among the largest birds that has ever lived. However, as narrator Sam Neill reveals, against all the odds, one giant bird from primeval New Zealand has survived to this day(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny and Suzannah Lipscomb introduce an evening of music to celebrate the 200th anniversary of Queen Victoria's birth. Featuring the music of her favourite composer, Mendelssohn, including his 'Scottish' Symphony and First Piano Concerto, performed by Stephen Hough on Victoria's own piano, loaned by Her Majesty The Queen from the Royal Collection. There's also a suite from Arthur Sullivan's ballet Victoria and Merrie England and songs composed by Prince Albert(n)

programme.title: Swallowed by the Sea: Ancient Egypt's Greatest Lost City
programme.desc: In the fading days of the pharaohs, Heracleion was the gateway to Egypt and a port beyond compare, but around 1,200 years ago a mysterious subsidence led to it being consumed by the Mediterranean. In 2000, archaeologists discovered the city's pristinely preserved remains 6km off the Egyptian coast and only 10 metres underwater. This documentary follows a team investigating the site, trying to find what caused this sacred city to plunge into the sea and why its inhabitants deliberately sank nearly 70 ancient warships. Presented by Lucy Blue(n)

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter continues his examination of how the car became an everyday part of people's lives, exploring the weird world of small vehicles. He discovers how austerity and fears of congestion led to European disasters in the shape of Britain's infamous three-wheelers, French death traps and German absurdities, and also takes to the battlefield to settle one of the greatest rivalries in car history(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.sub-title: Olympic Dreams of Russian Gold - Over the Limit - Storyville
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of the world's most outstanding rhythmic gymnast Margarita Mamun, the leading representative of the Russian national team. The film presents a groundbreaking year in her life, from the 2015 World Championships to the 2016 Olympic Games, offering unprecedented access to the hidden world of elite gymnasts and the challenging training regime demanded by the Russian system(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Muffy, Buster, and Binky argue over the design for a new mini golf hole(n)

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: The town celebrates Pieface Day in his honour after he rescues Walter and Bertie from danger(n)

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: A jam monster springs to life in Danger HQ when Squawk's experiment goes awry - and with DM and Squawk busy competing for Penfold's favour no one has noticed. With the voices of Alexander Armstrong and Kevin Eldon(n)

programme.title: MOTD Kickabout
programme.desc: A look ahead to the weekend's football fixtures(n)

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience(n)

programme.sub-title: Where There's a Wolf, There's a Way
programme.desc: Otis and Olympia stop werewolf Olaf from turning back into a boy(n)

programme.title: The Deep
programme.desc: Pirate boy Smiling Finn comes to the Nektons pleading for help(n)

programme.desc: Snotlout is rescued from a storm by a tribe of winged women, but his joy is short-lived as he realises he is deep trouble(n)

programme.title: The Zoo
programme.desc: A countdown of the top 10 clips from across the series, selected by the narrator and halfheartedly 'assisted' by Woody the Baboon(n)

programme.title: The Pets Factor
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Step Up to the Plate
programme.desc: The Kitchen Crew cook up a storm with their tropical-themed menu, while the Restaurant Crew try to make sense of the nonsense coming from difficult diners Marek Larwood and Inel Tomlinson(n)

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Contestants take on a recipe for squashed fly scones with home-made jam and cream, and also have to master the Showstopper Ombre cake to win a place in the quarter finals(n)

programme.title: Millie Inbetween
programme.desc: Mike tells a horrified Craig that he's planning to ask Mum to marry him, so Craig joins forces with Lauren to prevent it(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Eldon challenges Hunter to win back the Nationals Solo, and Emily is forced to prove her loyalty to Amanda by ending something that is just starting(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Thalia, Giselle and Amanda represent the group at a trio competition, while James tackles item 10 on Riley's list and gets to know Emily(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: James turns to the boys for help to solve a riddle for Riley, and the A-Troupe costumes for Nationals go missing(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: James gets his driving licence for Riley. Michelle searches for something she loves as much as dance and joins the debate team at school(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Emily tries to expose Amanda but gets caught in her own trap, while Kate discovers The Next Step's money is missing and holds Chloe responsible(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Amanda's true colours are exposed, and the results prove to be devastating. Emily catches West doing a good deed. Riley enlists James to convince a studio ex-member to come back(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Riley and James try to find their chemistry on the dance floor now that they are back together. Emily and West get to know each other, and Michelle's confidence receives a boost(n)

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: Choreographer Luther Brown drops in to help the boys with their small group routine. Thalia and Giselle are in a rush to try to teach Michelle the Nationals routine, while James gives Eldon dating advice. Reality-style drama, following the lives of a group of dancers as they attempt to win the regional championships(n)

programme.title: The Dumping Ground
programme.desc: Dumping Ground characters gather to watch videos made by Ashdene Lodge residents about their lives there. Featuring a mixture of new scenes and classic moments from The Dumping Ground and Tracy Beaker Returns. This time it's Floss' turn.(n)

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Historical characters and events are explored in sketches and quizzes based on the books by Terry Deary. Comedy talent including Sarah Hadland, Steve Punt and Meera Syal help bring the strange facts, gory battles and crazy inventions to life(n)

programme.title: The Dumping Ground
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Freaky Twins
programme.desc: The everyday lives and experiences of children living in a fictional care home. Drama based on books by Jacqueline Wilson, starring Connor Byrne and Kay Purcell(n)

programme.title: OOglies Funsize
programme.sub-title: The One with the Tomato Sawing in Half Magic Trick Talent Show
programme.desc: Toast soldiers take to the skies in their toast-rack helicopter, Maki and Iso ambush a nigiri in a Ninja Sushi attack and a stunt tomato performs a death-defying trick(n)

programme.desc: Dennis and the gang train Bertie in the art of standing up to people(n)

programme.title: The Dumping Ground
programme.sub-title: The Return of the Freaky Twins

programme.title: Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch
programme.sub-title: The Fresh Eggs of Bel Air
programme.desc: Tilly decides to set up a chicken farm in the family's Bel Air back garden(n)

programme.title: Class Dismissed
programme.desc: Following four year 8 pupils as they grapple with everyday life. In this edition, Dockbridge High is being inspected by Ofsted. Can the school pass with flying colours?(n)

programme.title: HH: Gory Games
programme.desc: Three contestants compete to win Year Spheres in games including Mummify Me and Gong Farming. Hosted by Dave Lamb and his assistant Rattus Rattus(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen sets out to to discover the truth about drink, featuring clips from the TV archives of pub acrobatics, thirsty camels and Mumbles Fizz(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: During the summer wedding season, Claire McCollum celebrates faith and marriage by visiting a couple in Cheshire whose wedding was fully organised by a church for just £1,000 thanks to Christian volunteers from the congregation. The Rev Kate Bottley meets members of the United Reformed Church, who three years ago voted to allow same-sex marriages in their churches, and a Christian same-sex couple who are able to marry in church as a result of this change(n)

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the 11th meeting of the Diamond League season, which comes from Alexander Stadium in Birmingham. Great Britain's Laura Muir will be looking for another victory in the 1500m after prevailing last time out in London, and American sprint star Christian Coleman is expected to feature in the 100m, having pipped home favourite Reece Prescott to the line last year(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following Ned, Sophie, Rowan and Sam Scharer as they take over as the wardens of Bardsey Island off the coast of off the Llyn Peninsula in Gwynedd, managing, renovating and caring for the island while hoping to become closer as a family in the process(n)

programme.title: Sam & Shauna's Big Cook Out
programme.desc: Samantha Evans and Shauna Guinn celebrate Cardiff's Gujarati community with a vegetarian cook-out, building a special giant multi-layered grill that several people can use at once to cook all the vegetables. The duo learn how to make an avocado and mango chutney using a recipe that has been handed down through the generations(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman will be hoping famously sharp minds will provide equally sharp answers as they host an academia-themed edition of the general knowledge quiz, in which points are earned by guessing the least likely correct answers given to questions posed to members of the public. Today's teams are made up of punk-rock poet John Cooper Clarke, an honorary doctor of the University of Salford, and historian Suzannah Lipscomb, a Lincoln and Baliol alumnus. TV archaeologist Mark Horton, a professor at University of Bristol, teams up with documentarian Alice Roberts, Professor of Public Engagement in Science at the University of Birmingham. Space scientist Maggie Aderin-Pocock joins forces with theoretical physicist Jim Al-Khalili of the University of Surrey, and University of Reading's Professor Kate Williams allies with The Infinite Monkey Cage comedian Robin Ince, who shares an alma mater with Kate in the form of Royal Holloway, University of London(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits the Lake District to take a look at some of the jobs working animals do. He joins the Cockermouth Mountain Rescue team to see search-and-rescue dogs in action and meets 15-week-old Jura, a border collie pup learning the ropes from the older dogs. He then meets George Newton and his two Dales ponies, Charlie and George. They work the forests for timber as they're able to get to places that normal machinery can't. But Sean is surprised to find that the biggest job is being done by the smallest creatures - beetles(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Helmingham Hall in Suffolk, where items include a sapphire ring and a sketch by Robert Baden-Powell, founder of the Boy Scouts and Girl Guides. Glass expert Andy McConnell challenges the owner of a collection of mid-18th century drinking glasses to identify the lone fake, while an intricate model of a butcher's shop intrigues Fergus Gambon(n)

programme.title: Poldark
programme.desc: Ross throws himself into fighting for Ned's liberty as George prepares to marry Cecily and finalise his deal with Hanson and Merceron. Geoffrey Charles is forced to make a desperate plan to elope with Cecily before the wedding, and Demelza uncovers a theft at the mine. Sam's growing attachment to Rosina comes under threat by Tess's claim to seek a purer life and with Ned's trial looming large and the odds stacked against them, Ross pleads for Dwight's help in a daring attempt to save his friend(n)

programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, which featured Sheffield United v Crystal Palace, and Chelsea v Leicester City. Plus, a round-up of all the goals and talking points from yesterday's matches(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas v Homophobia: The Legacy
programme.desc: The former rugby international continues his campaign to wipe out homophobia in football. With reported incidents of homophobic abuse at football matches at a record high, Gareth enlists the help of Parliament as he attempts to outlaw abuse in stadia. He also recruits Cardiff City, Leeds United and Norwich City in his campaign to tackle homophobia in the boardroom, the dressing room and on the terraces. All of this while coping with being the victim of a violent homophobic attack himself(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Fearsome Cuban crocodiles Fang and Keo have just taken up residence at Longleat Safari Park. Ben Fogle and Kate Humble reveal how the crocs' cousins in the wild are known for their leaping prowess - propelling themselves out of the water to reach heights of two metres to snatch prey from overhanging branches. Plus, tragedy strikes amongst the two rare wombats, the only ones of their kind in Europe(n)

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: Architect and university lecturer Michael Angus, Glasgow-based interior designer Anna Campbell-Jones and globally recognised lifestyle blogger Kate Spiers visit three homes in the east of Scotland. The judges begin with a renovated B-listed farmhouse on the outskirts of North Berwick, sporting a traditional exterior but contemporary interior. In Edinburgh, they find a wheelchair-accessible home with a smart design, then in Dalgety Bay see some radical transformations made to a simple 1970s bungalow(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stuart is alarmed to find Mick struggling to breathe and collapsed on the floor, while Max confronts Bobby for questioning him about Lucy. Sheree continues to make herself at home at the salon and suggests entering a hairdressing competition, much to Denise's annoyance(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Brighton & Hove Albion striker Glenn Murray, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey, Olympic swimming medallist Sharron Davies and three-time world snooker champion Mark Selby join host Sue Barker and team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Edith and Phil are determined to fulfill their dream of retiring to the sun together, but find themselves continually hampered by logistical difficulties. Edith is finding her irritating sister Joan very difficult to accommodate, and Roger, impatient to see Phil sell his house, decides to help the process along by checking his roof for dry rot. Wendy drops in on Edith and mentions that she is planning a children's book, and despite not yet knowing what the plotline will be, thinks it may involve a complicated family. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A secretive phone call from a solicitor to Mrs Brown leaves her children suspecting one of them may be adopted, while Father Quinn returns from rehab and asks Agnes to help out at his annual garden party - although as she proceeds to drive him crazy with a smartphone, his recovery may be short-lived. Meanwhile, Cathy receives a blast from the past, Rory and Dino have a bust-up, and Mark and Betty deliver bad news - they are leaving for Australia right away. Domestic comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A 79-year-old woman with dementia runs away from her care home and turns up at a south London police station to report a murder - sparking a reinvestigation into the mysterious disappearance of her police officer husband in 1956. The official report says that he fell into the river after one too many beers - but what secrets was this seemingly straight-as-a die old-fashioned bobby keeping? Meanwhile, Steve's son is arrested for buying weed, bringing the retired cop face to face with his ex-wife - a woman he hasn't seen in 10 years. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance from Julie Graham(n)

programme.title: Born Romantic
programme.desc: Three men and three women take dance lessons at a London salsa club. Fergus is looking for a second chance with Mo, whom he left at the altar, petty thief Eddie finds himself smitten with one of the dancers after a failed robbery, while ageing Frankie finds his charm is on the wane. Romantic comedy, starring Craig Ferguson, Olivia Williams, Jane Horrocks, Jimi Mistry, David Morrissey and Catherine McCormack(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the 11th meeting of the Diamond League season, which comes from Alexander Stadium in Birmingham. programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate the provenance of a painting with a Venetian view, trying to ascertain whether it is the work of Francesco Guardi or Michele Marieschi. Owner Nick Hopkinson inherited the painting from his great grandfather and Guardi and Marieschi are both known for their depictions of Venetian views and were contemporaries in the 18th century of Canaletto. But there's a big difference in value between them - if Nick's painting is a Marieschi it could be worth half a million pounds, if it's a Guardi it could be worth up to £10million(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman will be hoping famously sharp minds will provide equally sharp answers as they host an academia-themed edition of the general knowledge quiz, in which points are earned by guessing the least likely correct answers given to questions posed to members of the public. Today's teams are made up of punk-rock poet John Cooper Clarke, an honorary doctor of the University of Salford, and historian Suzannah Lipscomb, a Lincoln and Baliol alumnus. TV archaeologist Mark Horton, a professor at University of Bristol, teams up with documentarian Alice Roberts, Professor of Public Engagement in Science at the University of Birmingham. Space scientist Maggie Aderin-Pocock joins forces with theoretical physicist Jim Al-Khalili of the University of Surrey, and University of Reading's Professor Kate Williams allies with The Infinite Monkey Cage comedian Robin Ince, who shares an alma mater with Kate in the form of Royal Holloway, University of London(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption argues that judges - especially those of the European Court of Human Rights - have usurped power by expanding the interpretation of human rights law. But by contrast, the European Convention on Human Rights has become a dynamic treaty, taking on new interpretations and powers(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: From a location hidden beneath the Pennines, Julia sets off from the Cumbrian village of Dufton in search of one of Britain's best-kept secrets High Cup Nick, the so-called 'Grand Canyon of the North', a hanging glaciated valley 500 million years in the making. Along the route, she meets a family of Olympic rake makers, learns about the notorious Helm wind and meets Mike Hartley, the current record holder of the Pennine Way, who ran the 268 mile-trek in under three days(n)

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look at comedy in 2003, when Tim, Lee and Dawn's love triangle reached a memorable conclusion in The Office and the original run of Cold Feet bowed out with a tragic storyline. It was also the year when members of the public found themselves the unwitting comedy stooges of hidden-camera shows like Trigger Happy TV and Three Non Blondes, Bruce Forsyth made a memorable appearance as guest host of Have I Got News for You and comedy legend Bob Monkhouse performed his last ever stand-up gig to an audience of fellow comics in a small London pub(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The dead body of a teenage boy is found in a reservoir near a remote rural timber community. Vera and her team investigate, and discover that the boy had already been missing for three days prior to his death. However, the case is complicated by an apparent conspiracy of silence, as many of the townsfolk refuse to cooperate with the police investigation. programme.title: Hocus Pocus
programme.desc: A teenager in Salem, Massachusetts, breaks into a spooky house on Halloween and accidentally resurrects three witches who have been dead for 300 years. However, an old foe - a boy they turned into a cat - is on hand to help stop their mischief. Supernatural Disney comedy, with Bette Midler, Sarah Jessica Parker, Kathy Najimy and Omri Katz(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Mrs Roper is astonished and thrilled when George buys her an expensive present for their anniversary. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A spate of petty thefts is traced to a local nursing home, but Blaketon's investigations uncover something far more sinister. Meanwhile, David's first day in his new job goes spectacularly wrong, but Bernie is not surprised. Rural drama, starring Jason Durr and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alan is determined not to lower the standards of his restaurant. Melanie hears strange noises when she babysits in one of the cabins. Dan makes a fool of himself at the disco(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Raquel resents the way the McDonalds have taken over the pub, Andy returns to work a hero, and Mark asks Mike if he can stay the night as he does not want to be near Ken(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mark enjoys being with Mike and Alma, Derek lets Mavis read his dossier on Harry, and Raquel gets fed up with Liz ordering her around(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Change is in the air at the Abbey as an exciting opportunity for Alfred creates tension between Ivy and Daisy. Isobel appeals to Violet's sense of charity, but it doesn't come without a fight, while Branson considers a new future for himself and baby Sybbie. Raquel Cassidy (Teachers) joins the cast of the costume drama, with Matt Milne, Cara Theobold, Sophie McShera, Penelope Wilton, Maggie Smith and Allen Leech(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The beleaguered Cabot Cove residents fall victim to a callous development scheme - all except Jessica, who resolves to get to the bottom of the mystery. Angela Lansbury stars as the amateur sleuth, with William Windom, Ron Masak and Michael Horton(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When psychologist Reuben Beatty is murdered while moonlighting as a psychic, Lewis and Hathaway struggle to unravel the victim's baffling double life. Dark forces appear to be at work, and not just in the afterlife. Sanjeev Bhaskar guest stars, with Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Clare Holman and Rebecca Front(n)

credits.actor: Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Clare Holman, Rebecca Front

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The unassuming detective launches an investigation when a conscientious objector dies while in police custody, and the judge who ordered his arrest is nearly killed in his booby-trapped home. His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant(n)

programme.title: The Theory of Everything
programme.desc: Biopic of Stephen Hawking, exploring the renowned astrophysicist's romance with future wife Jane during their time at university in the 1960s and his initial diagnosis with motor neurone disease, which doctors believed would lead to his death within two years. Undaunted by deteriorating health, he continued his groundbreaking research into the origins of the universe. Starring an Oscar-winning Eddie Redmayne, with Felicity Jones and David Thewlis(n)

credits.actor: an Oscar-winning Eddie Redmayne, Felicity Jones, David Thewlis

programme.title: Cilla
programme.desc: It looks like Cilla has put that early botched audition and those disappointing record sales behind her as, following the success of Anyone Who Had a Heart, her new single You're My World becomes her second number one. However, while she may have conquered the UK charts, can she follow in the footsteps of her old mates the Beatles and crack America? The signs aren't looking good, and just to add to the pressure, her relationship with Bobby is becoming increasingly tumultuous. But as her manager Brian Epstein's private life starts to spiral out of control, it seems there could be an even bigger threat to Cilla's new-found success. The concluding instalment of Jeff Pope's biopic charting Cilla Black's rise to fame, starring Sheridan Smith, Aneurin Barnard and Ed Stoppard(n)

credits.actor: Sheridan Smith, Aneurin Barnard, Ed Stoppard

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Du Plessis notices a rift developing between Rosie and Max after their honeymoon and decides to help them settle their differences. A farmer captures an endangered and vicious wild dog, but when the expert called in to locate the pack turns out to be Alice's former partner, she is torn between giving their relationship another chance and staying at Leopard's Den with Danny. Stephen Tompkinson stars(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: The Leopard's Den residents are devastated when Du Plessis reveals he is moving to England to be with Caroline - especially Danny when he realises his friend's flight leaves the following day. Fatani buys a lion that will not come out of its cage, and when Rosie investigates she discovers a shocking case of negligence by the previous owner, so vows to do whatever is necessary to save the man's other animals. Deon Stewardson, Lucy-Jo Hudson and Stephen Tompkinson star(n)

credits.actor: Deon Stewardson, Lucy-Jo Hudson, Stephen Tompkinson

programme.desc: The Ropers babysit for their neighbours, but George misses his usual routine - so he nips out to a darts match with the infant in tow. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy(n)

programme.sub-title: It's What's on the Inside That Counts

Medical drama starring John Axon, Amy Robbins, Anna Madeley and Barbara Marten(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The mysterious death of a former police commissioner forces a reluctant Morse to team up with old professional rival Chief Inspector Dawson. As they and Sergeant Lewis begin to investigate, a link is uncovered to the unsolved case of a girl murdered 18 years previously. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Kenneth Colley and Christopher Eccleston(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall, Geraldine James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Millionaire Gordon Cloade's wife is widowed for the second time at the tender age of 23, arousing the suspicions of his impoverished relatives. Fanning the flames of envy and hatred, psychic aunt Kathy Cloade reveals the grieving widow's first husband is in fact still alive, and a second death prompts Poirot to delve deeper into the family's dark secrets. David Suchet stars, with Jenny Agutter, Tim Piggot-Smith and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy(n)

credits.actor: David Suchet, Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips, Raquel Cassidy

His inquiries uncover a web of lies and corruption in the heart of wartime Hastings. Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. When local woman Sonia Woodley is stabbed to death in the churchyard two years after the death of her husband, bespoke tailor Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate, and the village begins to reveal its secrets. Detective drama, guest starring James Wilby, Gwyneth Strong and Karl Davies (formerly Emmerdale's Robert Sugden), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

credits.actor: James Wilby, Gwyneth Strong, Karl Davies (formerly Emmerdale's Robert Sugden), John Nettles, Jason Hughes

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse(n)

credits.actor: Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd(n)

programme.desc: Joe sees Mark leaving Lisa's cabin first thing in the morning. Jack is furious when some cows break free from a field. Chris takes Kim and Frank for a meal in the Woolpack(n)

programme.desc: Elizabeth panics when her new kitten gets stuck down a drain. Joe catches Lorraine flirting with a camper. A drunken Michael shocks everyone at the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Samantha struggles to cope with the reality of her recent discovery and takes desperate action which leaves her life hanging in the balance as Nurse Taylor races to reach her. Frankie and Gordon attempt a daring rescue to help a teenager in danger on the weir, while Ken has something to hide from Harper. Rural hospital drama starring Anna Madeley, Scott Taylor and Robert Daws(n)

credits.actor: Anna Madeley, Scott Taylor, Robert Daws

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy chases joyriders tearing through Aidensfield and accidentally runs down Don Dibley's son during the pursuit. The boy's father demands compensation, but soon realises his problems have just begun as his daughter reveals she is pregnant. Steve finds himself in trouble and David invents a novel way to rid the estate of moles. Rural drama, starring Mark Jordon, James Carlton and Ted Robbins(n)

credits.actor: Mark Jordon, James Carlton, Ted Robbins

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceased's brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team come to the aid of nonagenarian Roberta and her 86-year-old best friend and carer Enid, who live together in a retirement bungalow in Wiveliscombe, Somerset. Originally from Liverpool, the two plucky pensioners have helped each other out countless times throughout the course of their friendship, which has lasted seven decades. However, they now find themselves trapped in their home by a treacherous slope in their garden, so Alan plans to set them free by designing a future-proofed garden in a vintage style(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The fatal poisoning of a deaconess who was about to become Oxford's first female chaplain makes Morse wonder whether the city's conservative clerics could be hiding a deadly secret. Further investigation uncovers a connection between the murder and a weight loss clinic. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately and Zoe Wanamaker (My Family)(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Adventure of the Clapham Cook
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian detective, who usually only accepts cases of national importance, bends his rules for once and finds himself in run-down Clapham. He investigates the disappearance of a local cook and becomes intrigued by the contents of the woman's trunk. David Suchet stars, alongside Hugh Fraser and Brigit Forsyth(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa helps out her ex-boyfriend Danny when he brings seven children from London to camp on Ruth's farm - supervised by Al and Bert - while she and Martin plan a date on the advice of Dr Timoney. However, their night at the restaurant is interrupted when Danny calls saying one of the youngsters has gone missing. Things get worse for the couple when Martin uncovers a health scare at James's first birthday party that makes all the guests leave very quickly. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian helps train Teddy, a blind cairn terrier who needs to learn basic commands to increase her chances of finding a new owner. He also meets posh pooch Luke, a rough collie who is used to the finer things in life after being spoilt in his previous home, and exhausted new mum Fifi is taken to be spayed. Having at least 10 puppies by the age of two has taken a physical toll on her, but there's a more worrying discovery in the operating theatre(n)

programme.title: Tonight at the Palladium
programme.desc: Jack Whitehall hosts the variety show, featuring an appearance by Simply Red, Leona Lewis performing Fire Under My Feet and Jess Glynne singing her number one single Hold My Hand. There's also comedy from Sara Pascoe and Britain's Got Talent finalist Jack Carroll, and more unusual acts such as Japanese dance troupe EL Squad from The Wrecking Crew Orchestra, and knife thrower Jack Dagger and his assistant the Unkillable Jenny(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A socialite is found dead next to the swimming pool of her manor house - following a series of threats against her. Barnaby and Troy are forced to dive deep into a dark world of adultery and deceit in affluent Midsomer Malham when they investigate. Mystery, starring John Nettles and Daniel Casey, with Charles Collingwood, Ann Bell and Miles Richardson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: The Theory of Everything

However, while she may have conquered the UK charts, can she follow in the footsteps of her old mates the Beatles and crack America? The signs aren't looking good, and just to add to the pressure, her relationship with Bobby is becoming increasingly tumultuous. But as her manager Brian Epstein's private life starts to spiral out of control, it seems there could be an even bigger threat to Cilla's new-found success.
