programme.desc: In Grasmere, chef Kevin Tickle goes foraging to cook some local dishes at his Forest Side restaurant, and in Wensleydale, gamekeeper David Hutchinson readies the estate for a shoot(n)

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect looks back at some of the most ingenious small-space projects with wings, wheels or on the waves. In Kent, George meets aeroplane enthusiast Vince, who paid £750 for the cockpit of a passenger jet and now plans to turn it into his dream summerhouse. In Lincolnshire, George comes faces to face with the UK's only 'phoenix camper', a stylish 1970s camper van with a retro games controller for a steering wheel(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Lost Family
programme.desc: Prince George makes a love match and marries the glamorous Princess Marina of Greece and Denmark, while Henry gives up the married Beryl Markham and also makes a suitable marriage. Edward, meanwhile, brings married American divorcee Wallis Simpson to George and Marina's pre-wedding party at Buckingham Palace, scandalising his parents. Not long after George V's Silver Jubilee celebrations, a unifying event for the nation, the King dies and Edward inherits the throne(n)

programme.title: The Handmaid's Tale
programme.desc: June must rely on an unpredictable Commander Lawrence to execute her plan, only for a terrifying journey to a place from her past to put everything in jeopardy. Serena Joy and Commander Waterford take a clandestine trip. Dystopian drama, starring Elisabeth Moss, Yvonne Strahovski, Joseph Fiennes and Bradley Whitford(n)

credits.actor: Elisabeth Moss, Yvonne Strahovski, Joseph Fiennes, Bradley Whitford

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.sub-title: Flights from Hell: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: With passenger numbers sky rocketing, airports and planes are increasingly becoming pressurised environments. Footage captured by people who were there highlights some of the most dramatic, extraordinary and unusual moments that UK passengers have experienced. From terrible behaviour in the terminal and tantrums on the tarmac to full-scale brawls on board, the programme hears from the people who were involved. Expert analysis from industry insiders and psychologists explains why people behave how they do on flights from hell(n)

programme.title: No Second Chance
programme.desc: Compassionate doctor Alice works in a hospital and is happily married to illustrator Laurent, with whom she has a six-month-old baby girl, Tara. But her contented life is turned upside down by a terrifying attack in her home that leaves her injured, her husband murdered and their infant daughter kidnapped. Two police detectives, Tessier and Romano, are assigned to the case, but they seem to suspect her of involvement. In French(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt heads to Italy to find out how supermarkets can sell pesto at an affordable price when the traditional ingredients are expensive, while Jimmy Doherty investigates why so many cockles in UK supermarkets come from abroad when he always thought of them as a quintessentially British staple. Kate Quilton takes a trip around Europe's largest marshmallow factory to find out why the sticky treat goes crispy and melty at the same time over the campfire(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Germany to learn the secret behind potato crisp, while Helen Lawal investigates whether carb-restrictive diets really burn fat quicker, and uncovers an eye-opening medical use for this popular weight-loss tool. Plus, Matt Tebbutt finds out the surprising reason why smoked fish is dyed yellow(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge follows the fortunes of Brit Mandy and her American business partner Steve, who have decided to build a swimming pool to entice more paying guests to their stunning cliff-edge chateau in the Dordogne. Desperate to increase income at his chateau, Michael invites Angel Adoree to help out with an experiment to do afternoon tea for the locals(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen present the halfway point of the patisserie contest, with the five remaining teams being challenged to prepare 24 opera slices and 24 exotic fruit roulades, before putting a modern spin on classic French pastry the religieuse. Judges Cherish Finden and Benoit Blin will then decide which duo is not going through to the next round(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole are close to the Malvern Hills, converting a pair of saddles into bar stools and hoping to make a profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Brigitte Tee, who runs four-star B&B Gorse Meadow in the New Forest, hosts the first round of the competition. Her guests are initially charmed by her eccentric personality, but the discovery of a bathroom window in need of repair and another room containing ladybirds, cobwebs and dust soon takes the shine off their stay(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to Lynton in north Devon, where they stay at Alistair and Emma Mundell's four-star Castle Hill Guesthouse. The pair only opened the B&B three months prior to the competition, and hope to impress with their recently renovated rooms, but Brigitte Tee proves hard to win over. Two of their guests are also forced to overcome their fears during a trip on a Victorian cliff railway(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves north to Blackpool and the three-star Vidella B&B run by John Downer and Neil Williams. The pair rig Blackpool-hater Alistair Mundell's room with practical jokes, and take their guests to Funny Girls drag cabaret bar, where Mark Crib confesses he has never had such a surreal time(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Mark Crib welcomes the group to the Urban Beach in Bournemouth, Dorset, for the week's final B&B visit. The boutique establishment features chic rooms with deluxe bathrooms, and the guests approve of the afternoon's activity - a speedboat ride along the Jurassic coast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Brigitte Tee is upset to discover she has been given only one point for cleanliness and a heated exchange results in a walk-out, before the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer revisit two sets of house-hunters who had very specific search criteria. Sisters Gemma and Lucy wanted two of everything in their potential new home in Reading, Berkshire, including parking spaces, bathrooms and equal-sized bedrooms, and professional historical architects Colin and Robin had an exacting list of requirements for Phil in Sussex(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Belinda and Cameron's home in the desirable Melbourne bayside suburb of Brighton happens to be on the wrong street - a very busy four-lane road. Andrew Winter thinks that a move closer to the city for this sophisticated couple is the right lifestyle choice, but Neale Whitaker believes that with a design adjustment, he can turn their cottage into a dream for a couple who love to entertain(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: With numbers in the wild dwindling rapidly, Chester Zoo's black rhino breeding programme is a key part of preserving the species. Black rhino Kitani is introduced to new mate Magadi, but with injury a high risk during mating, keeper Babs is concerned that Kitani may struggle to defend herself, since she lost her horn after the death of her calf. Meanwhile, there's a surprise birth in the orangutan house, and older sibling Tuti has to fend for herself and set out on the road to independence(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: This time, Take That's Mark Owen brings his 18-month-old Doberman, who needs treatment on a limp, into the surgery. Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel transforms the attic into a studio and storage space for her vintage treasures and takes Dorothy on a trip to Paris, while Dick has his own plans for repurposing some ancient tools he finds in the outbuildings. programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: The murder of Guglielmo Mantovani appears to be the tragic consequence of a burglary-gone-wrong. The man's three children - banker Leonardo, suffering artist Eva and eternal teenager Filippo - seem to be hiding something and none of them seems particularly distraught. Miriam Leone stars. In Italian(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A man is brought in with stomach pains and a suspected abdominal aortic aneurysm. His son receives a huge blow when he is told his father only has a 50 per cent chance of surviving the operation he urgently needs. A 33-year-old is treated after swallowing a cocktail of drugs in a suicide attempt, and staff fight to save a teenager who was stabbed in a street fight and has lost a life-threatening amount of blood(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A man with schizophrenia is admitted for the 19th time in a year after failing to take his diabetes medication. Meanwhile, a former betting-shop manager and a 16-year-old both suffer suspected strokes - the 61-year-old, once known as the 'memory man', is now coming to terms with his diminishing mental capabilities, while the teenager's identical twin and mother wait by his bedside for the results of a brain scan(n)

programme.title: The Supervet

Meanwhile, a four-year-old Coton-Tzu from Dublin with hip dysplasia may require a double hip replacement, and a Border Collie is brought in needing assistance with a problem with lameness(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ex-RAF officers Simon Stenning and Amanda Butcher have been visiting the Caribbean island of Antigua for several years, and now hope to find the perfect winter home there. Ben Hillman helps the pair, showing them five properties for their £330,000 budget(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton meets retired couple Denise Grahame and Roger Denis, who met three years ago, and developed a shared love for Bretagne culture. Moving to Brittany, the pair took up residence in a rented property near the town of Callac, but are now seeking to buy a place of their own. Laura is tasked with seeking out suitable homes within their budget of £120,000, and picks out five rural abodes she believes might fit the bill(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Music therapist Sarah Hadley from Surrey has a long-term love affair with the Greek island of Crete, a place she and her best friend Sue Williams have been visiting together for years. Now, Sarah is after her perfect Cretan holiday home and has asked Sue to help her with the decision. Jasmine Harman shows them five incredible properties for a budget of £150,000(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp meets Stuart and Lara, who want to be under the same roof after two years of being together, but living 100 miles apart. The pair look in the vicinity of Amersham, while Phil Spencer meets Randhir and Sheetal. They want to swap London living for village life in Berkshire before baby number two arrives in five weeks(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets a Suffolk family with a love of horses that plans to build an oak-framed cottage - but who are struggling to keep their modest budget under control(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Alston House in Cumbria, where owners Michael and Carole Allchorne are brimming with confidence, but Yvonne Copeland and Gail Wells are less than impressed with the decor. The guests are impressed with chef Michael's breakfast, but the hosts are left reeling by the criticisms levelled at their hotel during the feedback session(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Things are tense from the start as Carole and Michael from Alston House challenge Marie on her comments. Next, Marie from Scotia House wants to set the record straight with allegations of tit-for-tat scoring. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Zoe Bailey hosts the first dinner party in Shropshire, and the Zumba instructor is hoping to entertain with a game of gargling while humming a tune. She devises a menu featuring a souffle starter, which goes down well with her guests - artist Penny Timmis, travelling salesman Richard Mercer, radio DJ Paul Shuttleworth and Josh Sedgeley(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Artist and dairy farmer Penny Timmis hosts the second dinner party in Shropshire, hoping her dentist posing as a magician is enough to divert her guests' attention away from the possible animal hairs in their food(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: LED lighting salesman Richard Mercer hosts the third dinner party in Shropshire. He prepares a main course of paella, before suggesting a game of 'Guess the Gadget' to entertain his guests(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Youngest contestant of the week Josh Sedgley hosts the fourth dinner party from Shropshire. Some of the guests find his culinary efforts laughable, so he concentrates on the entertainment, which includes semi-naked butlers and a Las Vegas-style casino in his living room(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Traffic reporter and radio DJ Paul Shuttleworth hosts the final dinner party in Shropshire, and hopes that an evening of unusual outfits, fun food and a bouncy castle will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize. However, Paul's pork belly main doesn't live up to one guest's expectations(n)

programme.title: David Jason's Secret Service
programme.desc: The actor examines stories of heroism in the Second World War and explores a world of assassins, saboteurs, commandos, real-life super-villains and the ultimate femmes fatales. David also gets a taste of what it was like to be sent undercover as he takes flight in Britain's last remaining Lysander aircraft - the plane used to get agents in and out of Nazi-occupied Europe(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The latest batch of patients to be treated in the A&E department of London's King's College Hospital includes two with serious head injuries, including a cyclist from Brighton who has been airlifted to the building. Also brought in by helicopter is a father-of-four who fell from scaffolding as he repaired a roof, while other cases include former docker Bill, who has hurt his knee while drinking at his local working men's club(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The stories of children and teenagers brought into A&E. Sixteen-year-old Ellie has suspected spinal injuries after being thrown from her horse and crushed against a fence, while consultant Jacqui Butler treats four-year-old Destiny, who has a fishbone stuck in his tonsils from his lunchtime curry. Plus, a pregnant woman experiences intense pain following a sudden haemorrhage(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and David Mitchell take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Roisin Conaty. Tom Allen joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: A smooth-talking priest offers Ted a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to leave Craggy Island and run his own parish in Beverly Hills, but first he has to tell Jack, Dougal and Mrs Doyle that they are not invited. Final episode of the clerical comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, with Tommy Tiernan, Jeff Harding and a cameo appearance by music star Brian Eno(n)

credits.actor: Dermot Morgan, Tommy Tiernan, Jeff Harding, a cameo appearance by music star Brian Eno

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The first episode of the hit comedy. The Craggy Island cleric dreams of achieving stardom on the Faith of Our Fathers TV show - but his hopes are dashed by Jack and Dougal's untimely intervention. Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Frank Kelly star(n)

credits.actor: Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon, Frank Kelly

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the concluding part of his trip to the Mill Street Bistro in Norwalk, Ohio, Gordon's disagreement with proprietor Joe Nagy erupts into one of his most intense confrontations yet. Just when it looks like he has turned the corner and is ready to accept help, Joe slips back into his old ways, denying his own failings and picking petty fights. Can Gordon get this stubborn owner to finally accept his advice and secure the future of his restaurant?(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The latest batch of patients to be treated in the A&E department of London's King's College Hospital includes two with serious head injuries, including a cyclist from Brighton who has been airlifted to the building. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads to Sicily, where a vast network of tunnels leads to a subterranean salt-processing plant. Kate Quilton looks at free range hens, and is surprised to learn that most chicks on British farms have their beaks trimmed at birth. In the Netherlands, she visits a new cutting-edge farm designed using the latest scientific advances in hen welfare. Plus, in Scotland, Matt Tebbutt makes a surprising discovery about the origins of haggis, and then faces the ordeal of delivering some unwelcome news to an unsuspecting audience(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a couple in Worcestershire who have given up the rat race, selling their town house to buy some land in the country. With just £50,000 they set out to construct their own home from the most unlikely of starting points - four old portable office buildings. Schoolteacher Lauren, who discovered an enormous pond hidden under decking in the back garden, sets out to transform the plot into a beach-themed holiday retreat. As George's Italian road trip continues, he travels to the old town of Mathi, north of Turin, where he finds a modernist gem of a home(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps a couple looking for a home in Warrington, who do not seem to be able to agree on the best approach to house hunting, which has led to six months with no progress. In Liverpool, Phil Spencer comes to the aid of a successful entrepreneur looking for a house in Crosby, but who has very specific ideas(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

Kirstie is with single mother Anne, who sold her home in Sheffield to move to the countryside with her teenage son. However, a short-term stay with her parents has turned into eight months, so Kirstie has her work cut out to find Anne a suitable property fast. Phil is with Andrea and James, who are still searching for their ideal place after two years(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to The Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. Eventually, the winner is finally revealed(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Salesman Richard Massey hosts the first party in Colchester, hoping his cheeky-chappy demeanour and signature dish Drunken Duck will be enough to see him walk away with the £1,000 prize at the end of the week-long competition(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: PR worker Darius G Laws hosts the second dinner party in Colchester, but knows he will have to work hard after being critical the previous evening. He creates a menu filled with local ingredients and home-grown produce, including his own blackberry wine, and hopes his Roman dress code will be enough to win him the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: American army wife Adrena Austin hosts the third dinner party in Colchester. She hopes her simple menu, which revisits her Armenian heritage, will be a welcome change for her guests, but while reactions to the main course are mixed, her entertainment proves a resounding success(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: James Coe throws the fourth party in Colchester, and his choice of menu proves popular from the starter onwards. However, Jodie Ezekiel finds his incessant discussion of money somewhat off-putting. programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Vegetarian Jodie Edzekiel hosts the final dinner party in Colchester, and tries to please her guests by serving them meat, provided she can bring herself to touch it. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit is to the Ivybridge Hotel and Spa in Fishguard Bay, Pembrokeshire, where Cheryle and Anthony are unimpressed to be housed in an annexe(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to thrash out their issues. programme.title: Savage Kingdom
programme.desc: A small group of wild dogs seek a safe place to raise their six puppies, but faces a constant struggle to protect the offspring from predators(n)

programme.desc: A builder falls at work and dislocates his shoulder in such an unusual way it takes a five-strong team to pull it into place. Alan, who is in his sixties and had one leg amputated in 2009, comes in with the other dangerously swollen following a holiday in Goa, and 83-year-old James has to be persuaded by senior sister AnnMarie to accept treatment for a cyst on his knee(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: First-hand accounts of deadly wildfires in Paradise, California, that wiped out homes, towns and lives, telling the stories of the emergency responders and those who escaped(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The team examines a stash of rare motorcycle parts found behind a secret wall, and a propeller linked to Charles Lindbergh(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank attempt to buy from a handyman who lives on a unique homestead, but the handyman's deal making could end the pick before it begins(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In Detroit, Frank takes a big risk on one of the greatest pieces he has ever seen, and Mike acquires a Purple Heart along with a very special mission(n)

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad

programme.desc: Jon is joined by Morgana Robinson, Lolly Adefope and Matt Forde to address worries to do with The Future. What will we look like in the future? Will robots steal our jobs?(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets more tasks for comedians including Frank Skinner. He takes a boulder onto a train for a pleasant excursion. Then he, Roisin Conaty, Tim Key, Romesh Ranganathan and Josh Widdicombe try to make a Swedish man blush(n)

programme.title: Walter Tull - Forgotten Hero
programme.desc: Former EastEnders actor Nick Bailey investigates the story of Walter Tull, the pioneering black British footballer who died heroically fighting in World War One. He examines how Walter became the first black officer in the British Army when regulations required men of pure European descent, asks why he was denied the honour of a Military Cross for heroism and explores how he overcame racial abuse(n)

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: In the second programme, the historian explores how science and detection influenced the popular culture of murder during Victorian times. Writers including Charles Dickens and Wilkie Collins were fascinated by grisly crimes, and the literary genre that came out of it captured the imagination of readers. The presenter also reveals that when Jack the Ripper began his reign of terror in London at the same time Robert Louis Stevenson's Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde was published, the idea of the serial killer was introduced to the British public(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team visit the village of Beadnell on the Northumbrian coast, where mysterious fragments of human bone have emerged over recent years. Legend ties the area to local seventh-century Saint Ebbe, and it is widely believed that a 13th-century chapel once stood here. As the diggers set out to investigate possible remains of an earlier structure on the site, they unearth some shocking finds(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team head to Swansea to investigate the history of White Rock, which was one of the first copper-smelting works. As the archaeologists strip turf and shift tons of earth, they reveal the traces of a once-great industry and rediscover the story of the men who worked in it(n)

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates an account of the liberation of Paris and Brussels in 1944, events which to many signified the end of the war. However, as the German forces retreated, Poland fell victim to one of the most tragic events of the war, Documentary chronicling the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates theories that Hitler faked his suicide at the end of the Second World War and may have escaped from his Berlin bunker(n)

programme.title: Deep Wreck Mysteries
programme.desc: Divers explore the wreck of First World War battleship HMS Audacious, which was once the pride of the Royal Navy and thought to be 'unsinkable' until a single unexpected blow sealed its fate 14 miles off the coast of Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: The Life of Mammals
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the hunting tactics of the world's carnivores. Arctic foxes in the frozen north must collect food during warmer times to survive the winter, while the big cats of the southern hemisphere rely on stealth and surprise to catch each meal. The presenter also reveals how leopards and tigers are solitary hunters, coming together only to mate, whereas lions stay in family groups so they can tackle larger prey and better protect their young(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian and actor Billy Connolly sets out to fill in the gaps in his own family history. Starting in Glasgow, the city of Billy's birth, he hopes to find out more about his mother's relatives. He believes they hailed from Ireland, but records concerning his great-great-grandmother only deepen the mystery. Billy also heads to India, where he unearths details of the parts two of his ancestors played in historical events(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Four decades ago, Neasden-born model and actress Twiggy, then known as Lesley Hornby, found her life transformed when a reporter spotted her photo on the wall of a London hairdresser, and she was dubbed the 'face of the 60s'. Suddenly the 16-year-old was catapulted into life as a supermodel, travelling the world, mixing with some of its biggest stars and eventually appearing in films such as The Boyfriend and The Blues Brothers. In the 100th episode of the genealogy show, Twiggy examines her family's past, where she discovers a history involving strong women and a hint of crime(n)

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcock's films and Graham Greene's books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates what Hitler intended to do with the priceless works of art stolen by the Nazis from across Europe, as well as his obsession with religious artefacts(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston uncovers the truth behind James Bond, revealing what elements of the character were drawn from creator Ian Fleming's own experiences(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates the Dead Sea Scrolls, visiting the caves where they were discovered to find out who wrote them and why they were hidden(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates secret societies including the Freemasons, the Bilderberg Group and Bohemia Grove to try and uncover their political influence(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The wildlife on the great plains of Africa, Asia, North America and the Arctic, which cover a quarter of the land on Earth. The distinctive Tibetan fox is captured on film for the first time, while a herd of caribou migrates 2,000 miles across the tundra in search of grass. Night vision equipment tracks a pride of 30 lions hunting elephants in the tropical African savannah near one of the region's few remaining waterholes. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: An insight into woodlands around the world, from the stunted conifers cloaked in snow and ice in the Arctic forests, to the broadleaf woods in North America and Europe. Also featured are the 100m-high giant redwoods of California, as well as Madagascar's unusual upside-down trees, which store water in their swollen trunks and harbour strange wildlife. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough observes some of the varied species to be found in tropical rainforests, from the amusing courtship rituals of the birds of paradise and a chimpanzee group defending its territory to a flying-frog mating orgy and the sinister parasitic fungi cordyceps, which eat their victims alive(n)

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of guitarists taken from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Includes electric performances by Carlos Santana, Mark Knopfler, Peter Green and the Edge, and acoustic material by John Martyn, John Renbourn, Bert Jansch and Paco Pena(n)

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of 1970s-era axe-men, acoustic virtuosos and thumping riff merchants, in a compilation of guitar-heavy performances from the BBC TV archives. Guitar gods including Jimi Hendrix, Pete Townshend, Peter Green and Johnny Winter are joined by, among others, flamenco maestro Manitas De Plata, bottleneck bluesman Ry Cooder and straight-up rockers AC/DC and Thin Lizzy. Plus, Fleetwood Mac, the Jam and Be Bop Deluxe and Nils Lofgren(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: The Knights of the Garter arrive at Windsor Castle for their annual lunch with members of the monarchy, which includes a complicated procession through the palace. The royal archives allow cameras to view nearly 200 years of photo albums, including the thousands of photographs from the Queen Mother's collection, and the castle clock-maker faces a race to ensure all 450 timepieces on the estate are turned back(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Castle
programme.desc: The Duke of Edinburgh, in his capacity as Ranger of the Great Park, takes a tour of Windsor's grounds and points out the modifications he has made during his tenure, including a deer park and a farm shop selling local produce. He then competes in the Royal Horse Show, which also features Prince Harry. Meanwhile, the Prince of Wales and the Duchess of Cornwall host a special banquet for the Prince's Trust(n)

programme.desc: Inspector George Gently and The Professionals star Martin Shaw is the latest celebrity to go in search of his roots, and he's particularly interested in finding out more about his paternal grandfather Edwin, who was rarely spoken about by his family. The actor has only one photo of his ancestor, which shows him aged roughly 17 and wearing a military uniform. By zooming in on the picture, Martin's brother was able to identify their grandfather's badge, which suggests he was in the Royal Marines. Now, by meeting with a military historian, Martin tries to fill in the rest of the story, and in the process learns more about Edwin's role in defending Birmingham against German air assaults during the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: DJ, actor and presenter Reggie Yates sets out to trace his ancestry. His mother and father were both born in Ghana, but he never knew his grandparents, and he is keen to know where his surname comes from, believing it might be Dutch. However, as he looks further back, his family tree springs some unexpected surprises(n)

The second edition focuses on the decisions Jade Goody made which proved disastrous for her life in the spotlight, including a stint on Celebrity Big Brother which left her reputation and business in ruins after a series of abusive rows with Bollywood actress Shilpa Shetty triggered a huge wave of complaints about racist bullying(n)

programme.title: Jade: The Reality Star Who Changed Britain
programme.desc: Ten years on from her death, this series explores the rollercoaster life of Jade Goody, one of the most loved - and hated - reality TV stars, to tell a bigger story of class, politics and cultural change in Britain, at a time when reality TV became the biggest and most polarising form of entertainment in the country. The second edition focuses on the decisions Jade Goody made which proved disastrous for her life in the spotlight, including a stint on Celebrity Big Brother which left her reputation and business in ruins after a series of abusive rows with Bollywood actress Shilpa Shetty triggered a huge wave of complaints about racist bullying(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart McCracken and Elmore Victor head to Liverpool for what they expect to be a routine repossession, but instead face the most challenging eviction of their careers. Behind the front door, they find a critically ill two-year-old child with major heart problems, who is on constant life support and unable to breathe without a ventilator. Meanwhile, Brian O'Shaughnessy and Del Anglin chase £2,000 worth of debt owed by a business based in Kent, and recover £4,000 from a recycling business in Buckinghamshire. Plus, Stewart and Elmore encounter another family in crisis in Birmingham, where a retired couple who acted as guarantor for their daughter's tenancy are liable to pay over £3,000 after she defaulted(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Heidi

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: How a lifetime of study by developmental psychologist Uta Frith has transformed understanding of autism. The professor discusses the ways people with autism perceive and interact with the world, reveals how another kind of reality exists for them and explains why they often fail to understand jokes(n)

programme.title: Heyday TV
programme.desc: A retrospective review of Bowie's classic album, featuring extensive interviews with Woody Woodmansey and the late Trevor Bolder(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple from Luton search for a property in the Andalusia region of Spain, which must be big enough to accommodate their dachshund, parrot and collection of classic cars. With a budget of £85,000, they view six houses around the city of Cordoba, including a hillside residence with castle views and a place in town with a private swimming pool(n)

programme.title: Daddy Daycare
programme.desc: Nine men with limited childcare experience, who are either fathers or hope to have children soon, try to improve their skills by working in nurseries, taking on a variety of tasks such as nappy changing and supervising playtime. The first edition follows the efforts of three participants at a south London nursery - Garry, a workaholic whose wife thinks he does not have the patience to look after children, ex-military man Stefan, who takes a tough-love approach, and reluctant father-of-one Jay(n)

programme.title: Time Without Pity
programme.desc: An alcoholic writer returns home after years living abroad when he learns his estranged son has been convicted of murder and sentenced to hang. Although his attempts at reconciliation are rejected, he spends the last 24 hours before the execution trying to prove his son is innocent by identifying the real killer. Mystery, starring Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern and Peter Cushing(n)

credits.actor: Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern, Peter Cushing

programme.title: The Purple Plain
programme.desc: A Canadian squadron leader's increasingly strange behaviour begins to worry the officers of an RAF station deep in the Burmese jungle. There is further concern when the unstable pilot crashes his plane miles from the base, forcing him to make an exhausting trek back to safety. Second World War drama, starring Gregory Peck, Bernard Lee, Brenda de Banzi and Maurice Denham(n)

credits.actor: Gregory Peck, Bernard Lee, Brenda de Banzi, Maurice Denham

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: In the first of three documentaries focusing on health matters, Angela Rippon, who lost her mother to Alzheimer's, presents the latest scientific research about dementia - of which Alzheimer's is the most common form. She undergoes tests to see if she has any early signs, learns some surprising ways that may help prevent dementia and visits a number of people living with the disease. She also meets families that carry a gene for early onset Alzheimer's, discovering how they could be the best hope of finding a cure for this devastating disease(n)

programme.title: London Live News
programme.desc: News headlines, travel, weather and debate from across the capital(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Amanda Lamb helps Michael Grant search for a holiday home in Fuerteventura on the Canary Islands for £60,000. Properties on offer include a bargain flat, a traditional farmhouse and rural accommodation in the desert(n)

programme.title: Back from the Dead: The John Darwin Story
programme.desc: The story of John and Anne Darwin, who were sentenced to more than six years' imprisonment for an insurance fraud which saw the husband fake his own death in March 2002 and start a new life in Panama. The scheme was uncovered when John appeared at a police station in 2007 claiming to be a missing person suffering from amnesia(n)

programme.title: Make Bradford British
programme.desc: The sociological experiment concludes with the participants being paired up to learn about each other's backgrounds. A black man shows a retired policeman the estate where he once suffered a racially motivated attack, and a landlady invites a Muslim woman to work in a pub where the locals challenge her over her traditional attire. A welder comes away with a changed view of Islam after visiting one of the city's 80 mosques, while a self-confessed feminist invites taxi driver Mohammed to a classic British dinner party and questions him about his cultural views on women(n)

programme.title: Bonded By Blood 2: The Next Generation
programme.desc: A gangland shooting leaves the London underworld with a dangerous power vacuum. A thriving drug kingpin, a petty thief striving to be accepted and an unstable criminal are all determined to seize that power for themselves, tearing their friendship apart in the process. Crime drama, starring Sam Strike, Josh Myers and George Russo(n)

credits.actor: Sam Strike, Josh Myers, George Russo

programme.desc: A Brixton teenager's misguided attempts to attract the attention of a heartless wannabe rapper have terrible results. Drama, starring Jessica Sula, Lucien Laviscount and Ntonga Mwanza(n)

programme.title: War and Peace
programme.desc: Three Russian aristocrats - flighty Natasha, pacifist Pierre and military officer Andrei - endure a series of tumultuous events as Naopleon Bonaparte's armies invade their country. Historical drama based on Leo Tolstoy's novel, starring Audrey Hepburn, Henry Fonda and John Mills(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles' Other Mistress
programme.desc: Documentary film examining the life of Australian heiress Dale 'Kanga' Tryon, who was Camilla Parker Bowles' rival in the 1970s for the affections of the Prince of Wales. Expelled from the royal circle for speaking to the Press about her relationship with the Windsors, she suffered a breakdown that saw her sectioned under the Mental Health Act, and died in 1997(n)

programme.title: Welcome to the Mosque
programme.desc: Robb Leech provides an insight into what life is like behind a mosque's walls, from rituals and marriage, to racism experienced by older community members when they were growing up. He witnesses segregation and meets younger members of the community, who are afraid of 'free-mixing' with the opposite sex. He also captures the response to the news of the three London schoolgirls who left Britain to join Isis jihadists in Syria, including frantic phone calls and the journey to Istanbul with the girls' families to find them(n)

programme.title: Scramble
programme.desc: A man ends up in over his head in the world of organised crime when his girlfriend is kidnapped by an underground prostitution ring. His attempts to raise the ransom money land him a job delivering dead bodies for a crime syndicate. Crime thriller, starring Brett Newton and Victor Kelso(n)

programme.title: Love, Honour and Obey
programme.desc: A humble courier manages to wheedle his way into the confidence of north London's toughest mobsters. Disappointed by the lack of action, he sets out to make things more interesting - but inadvertently sparks a bloody gangland conflict. Comedy thriller, starring Jude Law, Sadie Frost, Jonny Lee Miller, Ray Winstone, Rhys Ifans, Kathy Burke and Sean Pertwee(n)

credits.actor: Jude Law, Sadie Frost, Jonny Lee Miller, Ray Winstone, Rhys Ifans, Kathy Burke, Sean Pertwee

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple from Luton search for a property in the Andalusia region of Spain, which must be big enough to accommodate their dachshund, parrot and collection of classic cars. With a budget of £85,000, they view six houses around the city of Cordoba, including a hillside residence with castle views and a place in town with a private swimming pool(n)

programme.title: London Live Sessions
programme.desc: Intimate performances by London's up-and-coming bands, featuring You Me at Six(n)

programme.title: News Rewind
programme.desc: A digest of news and events from across London from the past few days(n)

programme.title: Talking Points
programme.desc: Interviews on news and events from across London(n)

programme.title: Roman Britain from the Air
programme.desc: The Romans were the first to invade and occupy Britain and their architecture and way of life have left a legacy that has lasted 1,600 years. Christine Bleakley takes to a helicopter to view remains that are best seen from the air, while historian Michael Scott is on the ground to explore further what each sky-high vantage point reveals. Their journey takes them from London, across to Wales and north to Hadrian's Wall, visiting fascinating sites that include the best-preserved Roman legionary barracks anywhere in Europe, the 6,000-seat amphitheatre at Caerleon and town walls that stretch for more than a mile(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Zilpah Hartley helps a newlywed couple find their perfect Caribbean retreat in the Cayman Islands. Husband John has built and designed his own home in the past, and brings his architectural know-how to bear on the assortment of apartments, plantation houses and studios they inspect(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Victoria Hollingsworth helps a mother of twins and her best friend find their ideal holiday home in the Canary Islands. Their budget of £100,000 allows them to consider a bungalow with a beach view, a modern villa and a detached house on a new complex(n)

programme.title: Mud Men
programme.desc: Steve Brooker and Johnny Vaughan search for hidden treasures along the Surrey Quays. After finding a 19th-century bottle, they mix Victorian cocktails(n)

programme.title: Time Without Pity
programme.desc: An alcoholic writer returns home after years living abroad when he learns his estranged son has been convicted of murder and sentenced to hang. Although his attempts at reconciliation are rejected, he spends the last 24 hours before the execution trying to prove his son is innocent by identifying the real killer. Mystery, starring Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern and Peter Cushing(n)

credits.actor: Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern, Peter Cushing

programme.title: London Live Sessions
programme.desc: Jason Mraz gives an intimate performance in a London venue(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles' Other Mistress
programme.desc: Documentary film examining the life of Australian heiress Dale 'Kanga' Tryon, who was Camilla Parker Bowles' rival in the 1970s for the affections of the Prince of Wales. Expelled from the royal circle for speaking to the Press about her relationship with the Windsors, she suffered a breakdown that saw her sectioned under the Mental Health Act, and died in 1997(n)

programme.title: Operation Cupid
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Charles Farrell as a Cockney wide boy running a struggling matrimonial agency. When a wealthy widow turns up looking for a husband, he sees the chance to become rich by posing as a South African millionaire and romancing her himself. With Avice Landone and Wallas Eaton(n)

programme.title: Hell is Sold Out
programme.desc: An author long assumed dead makes a surprise return, only to find books he did not write are being published under his name. He discovers that another writer, claiming to be his widow, is passing off her own creations as his previously unseen work to get them published. Drama, starring Mai Zetterling, Herbert Lom and Richard Attenborough(n)

credits.actor: Mai Zetterling, Herbert Lom, Richard Attenborough

programme.title: Welcome to the Mosque
programme.desc: Robb Leech provides an insight into what life is like behind a mosque's walls, from rituals and marriage, to racism experienced by older community members when they were growing up. He witnesses segregation and meets younger members of the community, who are afraid of 'free-mixing' with the opposite sex. He also captures the response to the news of the three London schoolgirls who left Britain to join Isis jihadists in Syria, including frantic phone calls and the journey to Istanbul with the girls' families to find them(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad(n)

programme.title: London Live Book Club
programme.desc: Not sure what to read next? We offer inspiration as we chat to London-based writers, and those who feature the capital in their works(n)

programme.title: Spotlight London
programme.desc: Interviews about arts and events showcasing London's vibrant culture scene(n)

programme.title: Mud Men
programme.desc: Steve Brooker and Johnny Vaughan hunt for medieval and Tudor treasure at Fulham Palace, and get an archery lesson from actor Robert Hardy(n)

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen
programme.desc: A former Army colonel, angry at his enforced retirement, sets out to exact an elaborate revenge on society in general. Recruiting seven other former servicemen who have turned to a life of crime, he plans a major bank robbery with military precision. Crime caper, starring Jack Hawkins, Nigel Patrick, Richard Attenborough, Roger Livesey and Bryan Forbes(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
